title: Extract Public Calculator Service Tasks
principles: Keep calculators reusable across HTTP, SDK, CLI and in-process callers.
Keep HTTP handlers thin transport adapters over Effect service methods.
Keep core deterministic and lower level than calculator orchestration.
Reuse canonical schemas, descriptors, tagged errors, services, scenario layers, rule-pack layers and report schemas from owning packages.

MUST use Option, Match and schema-owned optional fields for optional request and response policy; do not use raw undefined branching, raw null comparison, conditional object spreads or jurisdiction-specific defaults.
MUST use pipe-first composition for sequential calculator transformations and Effect pipelines; do not hide data flow inside nested wrapper calls.
MUST expose public calculation facts as a union of canonical calculator input schemas, not Schema.Unknown.
MUST validate calculation facts again against the selected calculator catalog entry so incompatible calculator/facts combinations return calculator-specific help.
Preserve public route behavior and generated OpenAPI shape while moving ownership.

globalVerification.commitPolicy: Commit only after each coherent implementation slice passes verification, Changeset review and architecture review.
requiredBeforeFinalPR: bun run changeset or explicit no-changeset rationale
OpenAPI JSON still includes the public calculator metadata and calculate routes
OpenAPI JSON for POST /api/v1/calculators/{calculatorId}/calculate exposes the union of supported canonical calculator facts schemas
API smoke checks for metadata route, successful calculation route and schema-guided validation error
Architecture audit against docs/architecture/package-ownership.md, package-boundaries.md, effect-services.md and api-and-sdk.md
evidenceRequired: Changeset path and release-train impact, or no-changeset rationale
OpenAPI request-body evidence showing supported public calculation fact schemas are not Schema.Unknown

Effect-native optionality and primitive audit showing no payload.jurisdiction ?? "AU", raw null/undefined checks, conditional help object spreads, nested wrapper transformations, inline schema issue path probes, native array pipelines, native Map/Set, thrown exceptions, async/await/new Promise, JSON.parse/stringify or hidden Date/Math.random usage remain in calculator service code
Dependency-direction audit showing @whattax/calculators does not import @whattax/http-api or app modules

tasks.title: Create the calculators package shell and ownership docs
tasks.goal: Create @whattax/calculators as a workspace package with package docs, exports and build/typecheck wiring, without moving runtime behavior yet.
tasks.implementationPrompt:
You are implementing exactly one task from the WhatTax spec/task list.

Before editing:

- Read the target spec, this task object, relevant current files, and the relevant docs in `docs/architecture/*`.
- Identify the owning package for every schema, type, ID, service, layer and tagged error you touch.

Implementation rules:

- MUST use Effect-native primitives when they fit: `Data`, `Schema`, `Array`, `Chunk`, `HashSet`, `HashMap`, `Match`, `Context`, `Layer`, `Config`, `Service`, `Record`, `Result`, `Exit`, `Bun`, `Platform`, `Command` and `ManagedRuntime`.
- Do not fall back to plain TypeScript control flow, nullable values, mutable records, mutable arrays, `switch`, `Object.values`, `Object.entries`, ad hoc `_tag` objects, unsafe casts, stringly branching, custom env parsing or hand-rolled runtime wrappers when an Effect/schema/platform primitive fits.
- Reuse canonical schema-derived types, branded IDs, tagged errors, service contracts and constructors from the owning package.
- Do not create mirrored DTOs, local duplicate types, local duplicate schemas or transport-only shape copies when an owning schema or API/SDK contract already exists.
- Keep one-off Effect error handling and transformations inline at the callsite. Do not extract tiny mapper/wrapper helpers for single-use `Effect.mapError`, `Effect.catchTag`, `Effect.catchAll` or `Effect.catchAllDefect`.
- Browser/runtime code must consume browser-safe API/SDK exports instead of importing server-only internals.
- Package-facing changes MUST add or update a Changeset with `bun run changeset`, using a user-facing changelog summary. If the task is not package-facing, state that explicitly in the handoff.

Verification and handoff:

- Run this task's mandatory verification gates, including `bun run verification` unless the task explicitly documents a narrower gate.
- Report the Changeset path and release-train impact, or report why no Changeset was required.
- Report changed files, verification commands, outcomes and residual risks.
- Do not start or delegate another task. The parent agent must review, audit, verify and explicitly accept this task before the next task begins.

Task-specific scope: create `packages/calculators` package files, README and exports only. Do not move handler logic yet.
outputs: packages/calculators/package.json
packages/calculators/README.md
packages/calculators/tsconfig.json
packages/calculators/tsconfig.build.json
packages/calculators/src/index.ts
Package exports that do not expose HTTP or app runtime modules
Changeset covering the new package shell
mandatoryVerification: Create or retain lint coverage proving the calculators package is checked by scoped WhatTax Oxlint rules
Search packages/calculators for imports from @whattax/http-api, apps/api, apps/web and runtime.server/runtime.client
completionCriteria: @whattax/calculators is a workspace package with explicit exports
The package README states ownership and dependency boundaries
No runtime behavior moved yet
Parent agent verified package ownership alignment
Changeset covers the new package

tasks.title: Move calculation execution and expected error shaping into service methods
tasks.goal: Make @whattax/calculators own public calculator service methods so HTTP handlers become thin wrappers.

tasks.title: Tighten public calculation facts schemas
tasks.goal: Replace the generic public calculate facts contract with a union of canonical calculator input schemas while keeping selected-calculator validation and help in the service layer.
tasks.implementationPrompt:
You are implementing exactly one task from the WhatTax spec/task list.

Before editing:

Task-specific scope: make `PublicCalculationFacts` a union of canonical rule-owned calculator input schemas such as `TakeHomeScenarioInputSchema` and `AnnualTaxScenarioInputSchema`; add an `inputSchema` reference to each calculator catalog entry; decode facts with the selected entry's input schema inside `PublicCalculatorService.calculate`; keep `POST /api/v1/calculators/:calculatorId/calculate` as the public route.
outputs: PublicCalculationFacts is a Schema.Union of canonical calculator input schemas from owning rule packages
PublicCalculationRequest.facts uses PublicCalculationFacts instead of Schema.Unknown
Each CalculatorCatalogEntry references the canonical input schema accepted by that calculator
PublicCalculatorService.calculate decodes request.payload.facts with the selected entry inputSchema before execution
Incompatible calculator/facts combinations return CalculatorInputDecodeError with descriptor-backed help for the selected calculator
HTTP API OpenAPI output shows supported calculator facts schemas for the generic calculate route
Effect Vitest coverage for successful service calculation, HTTP in-process calculation and incompatible facts errors
mandatoryVerification: Inspect /api/docs/openapi.json and confirm POST /api/v1/calculators/{calculatorId}/calculate requestBody exposes the canonical facts union instead of Schema.Unknown
Smoke check a valid annual-tax facts payload sent to au.pay.take-home returns CalculatorInputDecodeError with pay-calculator help
Search packages/calculators/src and packages/http-api/src for `facts: Schema.Unknown`
browserVerification: Open /api/docs and confirm the calculate request body shows supported fact shapes if a browser tool is available; otherwise record generated OpenAPI evidence
completionCriteria: The public calculate route remains generic by calculatorId
Generated API docs expose concrete supported fact shapes
The service still owns selected-calculator compatibility and descriptor-backed help
No canonical fact schemas are mirrored or locally redefined
Parent agent reviewed OpenAPI, service decode flow and tests before accepting
